2604.16009.title: MEDLEY-BENCH: Scale Buys Evaluation but Not Control in AI Metacognition
2604.16009.paper_summary: Metacognition, the ability to monitor and regulate one's own reasoning, remains under-evaluated in AI benchmarking. We introduce MEDLEY-BENCH, a benchmark of behavioural metacognition that separates independent reasoning, private self-revision, and socially influenced revision under genuine inter-model disagreement. The benchmark evaluates 35 models from 12 families on 130 ambiguous instances across five domains and reports two complementary scores: the Medley Metacognition Score (MMS), a tier-based aggregate of reflective updating, social robustness, and epistemic articulation, and the Medley Ability Score (MAS), derived from four metacognitive sub-abilities. Results show a robust evaluation/control dissociation: evaluation ability increases with model size within families, whereas control does not. In a follow-up progressive adversarial analysis of 11 models, we observed two behavioural profiles, i.e., models that revise primarily in response to argument quality and models that track consensus statistics. Under within-model relative profiling (ipsative scoring), evaluation was the weakest relative ability in all 35 models, indicating a systematic knowing/doing gap. Smaller and cheaper models often matched or outperformed larger counterparts, suggesting that metacognitive competence is not simply a function of scale. These findings position MEDLEY-BENCH as a tool for measuring belief revision under social pressure and suggest that future training should reward calibrated, proportional updating rather than output quality alone.
2604.16009.paper_summary_zh: 元認知，即監控和調節自己推理的能力，在人工智慧基準測試中仍然被低估。我們介紹了MEDLEY-BENCH，這是一個行為元認知的基準，將獨立推理、私密自我修正和在真正的模型間分歧下的社會影響修正分開。該基準在五個領域中對來自12個家族的35個模型進行了130個模糊實例的評估，並報告了兩個互補的分數：元合成元認知分數（MMS），這是一個基於層級的反思更新、社會穩健性和認識論表達的綜合指標，以及元合成能力分數（MAS），源自四個元認知子能力。結果顯示出一種穩健的評估/控制分離：在家族內，評估能力隨著模型大小的增加而增加，而控制則沒有。在對11個模型進行的後續漸進對抗分析中，我們觀察到兩種行為特徵，即主要根據論證質量進行修正的模型和跟踪共識統計的模型。在模型內相對剖析（自我評分）下，評估是在所有35個模型中最弱的相對能力，顯示出系統性的知識/行動差距。較小且成本較低的模型往往與較大的對應模型匹配或超越，這表明元認知能力並不僅僅是規模的函數。這些發現使MEDLEY-BENCH成為在社會壓力下衡量信念修正的工具，並建議未來的訓練應該獎勵經過校準的、比例更新，而不僅僅是輸出質量。
2604.16009.author: Farhad Abtahi et.al.
2604.16009.authors: Farhad Abtahi, Abdolamir Karbalaie, Eduardo Illueca-Fernandez, Fernando Seoane
